metric_value: Modular yet integrated security framework: VirtualVault (trusted gateway) + Authorization Server (business-logic) + ICF (crypto framework) + ImagineCard (authentication); business-logic-driven vs mechanical ACL approach
source_page: HP Praesidium — A Family of Products

metric_value: B1 class SecureWare; protected reference monitor isolates web-server from Intranet resources; roles-based admin eliminating superuser; real-time audit log monitoring; already deployed by Federal banks for Internet banking
notes: Based on SecureWare technology acquired by HP from National Computer Security Center evaluation

metric_value: Role-and-rules-based authorization separating identity/authorization from back-end; authorized access by app/app-server/location/time-of-day/day-of-week/roles; secured replication for HA
Designed to resolve national encryption export control impasse; enables enterprises to use politically-approved crypto per political boundary; HP-proposed open standard
IS executives testing small-scale pilot projects between trading partners; point-solutions found inadequate; internal-commerce-networks physically disconnected from corporate network for security
notes: Aberdeen field research findings on enterprise e-commerce readiness

metric_name: Integrated Security Framework vs Point Solutions
metric_value: Aberdeen concludes point-solutions will not work for production-grade enterprise e-commerce; integrated framework required; Praesidium approach will prevail

metric_name: Integrated Security Framework vs Point Solutions Outcome
metric_value: Prediction verified: enterprise security market consolidated around integrated frameworks; HP Praesidium products were discontinued but the framework concept prevailed — IAM platforms (RSA, IBM Tivoli, Microsoft IAM) and unified security suites became the industry standard by 2005
notes: HP VirtualVault replaced by HP-UX 11i trusted computing; broader framework concept confirmed

metric_name: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) vs ACL Prediction
metric_value: Aberdeen predicts role-and-rules-based authorization will replace mechanical ACL-based security controls; transforming mechanical controls into business-logic

metric_name: RBAC vs ACL Adoption Actual Outcome
metric_value: RBAC became the dominant enterprise authorization model; NIST RBAC standard published 2004; RBAC is now the industry standard for enterprise IAM, supplanting traditional ACLs as Aberdeen predicted
notes: NIST SP 800-63 and enterprise IAM standards confirm RBAC dominance

metric_value: Praesidium plans to integrate with SET for credit card transactions; SET and X.509 cited as key authentication standards to support

SET protocol was abandoned by 2001; SSL/TLS became the universal payment security standard instead; 3D Secure replaced SET. Aberdeen's implicit preference for flexible crypto frameworks was correct but SET itself failed.
notes: SET was competing standard that lost to SSL; Aberdeen's general security architecture predictions were better than specific standard predictions

metric_value: ImagineCard may provide features needed for universal adoption as a multi-credential smart card for enterprise and consumer authentication

ImagineCard did not achieve universal adoption; smart card adoption in enterprise remained limited until PKI/FIDO standards era; consumer smart card payments (EMV chip) achieved mainstream adoption but through Visa/Mastercard not HP framework
Praesidium could reduce or eliminate $300,000 up-front cost to establish account with third-party CA; enterprises with large transaction volumes can run own secured back-end operations
notes: HP claim; independent CA market later dominated by VeriSign/DigiCert

metric_value: HP Praesidium could do for commercial cyber-transactions what vaults did for banks; provides tools for IS executives to secure Intranet and conduct Internet commerce
notes: Colorful Aberdeen endorsement of HP's security vision

metric_value: Praesidium framework intentionally modeled on HP OpenView's successful network management framework approach; ISV partner recruitment strategy parallels OpenView ecosystem development
source_page: The Key to Success — Frameworks, Business and Software Partnerships

metric_value: Credentials: passwords, smart cards, X.509 certificates, biometrics; ImagineCard for multi-credential; HP integrating diverse credential types including SET and public-key systems
ICF resolves national encryption export control impasse; enables per-country crypto policy; protects credentials and message traffic in transit over wire and wireless networks
B1-class trusted gateway isolating web apps from Intranet; roles-based admin; real-time audit; goes beyond firewall protection; shields against internal threats and external
